caption: Network Activity
description:
Network Activity captures connection lifecycle events and application-layer protocol observations in a single, unified event class.

How it works:

  • Use activity_id for the connection lifecycle only: Open, Close, Reset, Fail, Refuse, Traffic, or Listen.
  • Identify the Layer 7 protocol with network_application_protocol_id (e.g. HTTP, DNS, DHCP).
  • Activate the matching network profile in metadata.profiles (e.g. network_http, network_dns) to unlock protocol-specific fields.
  • Protocol-specific details (HTTP method, DNS query type, DHCP message type) go in their profile attributes — not in activity_id.

Example flows:

1. HTTP request/response: Set activity_id = 6 (Traffic), network_application_protocol_id = 1 (HTTP), activate profile network_http, then populate http_request and http_response.

2. DNS lookup: Set activity_id = 6 (Traffic), network_application_protocol_id = 2 (DNS), activate profile network_dns, then populate dns_query and dns_answer.

3. TCP connection open: Set activity_id = 1 (Open), populate src_endpoint and dst_endpoint with IP/port, and connection_info with protocol details.

4. Firewall block: Set activity_id = 5 (Refuse), populate endpoints and connection_info. Add disposition via the security_control profile if applicable.

This class replaces the former protocol-specific classes: http_activity, dns_activity, dhcp_activity, ftp_activity, smb_activity, ssh_activity, ntp_activity, rdp_activity, and tunnel_activity.

v2.0 Breaking Change — type_uid: The former per-protocol class_uid values (e.g. 4002 for HTTP, 4003 for DNS) no longer exist. All network protocol events now share class_uid = 4001. To identify the protocol, use network_application_protocol_id instead of class_uid or type_uid.
name: network_activity

activity_id.description: Connection or flow lifecycle only. Do not encode HTTP methods, DHCP message types, or other L7 verbs here—use the protocol profile fields (e.g. http_method_id, dhcp_message_type_id).

1.caption: Open
1.description: A new network connection was opened.

3.caption: Reset
3.description: The network connection was abnormally terminated or closed by a middle device like firewalls.

4.caption: Fail
4.description: The network connection failed. For example a connection timeout or no route to host.

5.caption: Refuse
5.description: The network connection was refused. For example an attempt to connect to a server port which is not open.

6.caption: Traffic
6.description: Network traffic report for an established or connectionless flow.

7.caption: Listen
7.description: A network endpoint began listening for new network connections.

dst_endpoint.description: The responder of the network connection. In some contexts an event source cannot correctly identify the responder. Refer to is_src_dst_assignment_known for certainty.

network_application_protocol_id.description: Normalized L7 protocol for this observation. Drives which optional profile attributes are populated.

network_type_uid.description: A computed identifier combining the L7 protocol and connection lifecycle activity: network_application_protocol_id * 100 + activity_id. Enables single-value filtering for protocol-specific activities (e.g. HTTP Traffic = 106, DNS Traffic = 206, SSH Open = 601).

protocol_context.description: Extension point for protocols without a first-class profile.

src_endpoint.description: The initiator of the network connection. In some contexts an event source cannot correctly identify the initiator. Refer to is_src_dst_assignment_known for certainty.

url.description: The URL details relevant to the network traffic.

references.description: OCSF issue #1384 — Profiles for Network Activity
